joined_reference: it confirms fincher 's status as a film maker who artfully bends technical know-how to the service of psychological insight .  too much of it feels unfocused and underdeveloped .  a great ensemble cast ca n't lift this heartfelt enterprise out of the familiar .  prurient playthings aside , there 's little to love about this english trifle .  it moves quickly , adroitly , and without fuss ; it does n't give you time to reflect on the inanity -- and the cold war datedness -- of its premise .  it proves quite compelling as an intense , brooding character study .  bogdanovich tantalizes by offering a peep show into the lives of the era 's creme de la celluloid .  looks and feels like a project better suited for the small screen .
